001/* -*- mode: Java; c-basic-offset: 2; indent-tabs-mode: nil; coding: utf-8-unix -*-

009 *
010 * Unless required by applicable law or agreed to in writing, software distributed under the License is distributed on
011 * an "AS IS" BASIS, WITHOUT WARRANTIES OR CONDITIONS OF ANY KIND, either express or implied. See the License for the
012 * specific language governing permissions and limitations under the License.
013 */
014package org.microbean.event;
015
016import java.util.SequencedSet;
017
018import javax.lang.model.element.Element;
019
020import org.microbean.assign.Annotated;
021import org.microbean.assign.Aggregate;
022
023import org.microbean.bean.ReferencesSelector;
024
025/**
026 * An {@link java.util.EventListener EventListener}, and an {@link Aggregate} that {@linkplain #eventReceived(Object,
027 * ReferencesSelector) receives} <dfn>events</dfn>.
028 *
029 * @param <R> the result of reception; almost always {@link Void}
030 *
031 * @param <E> the event type
032 *
033 * @author <a href="https://about.me/lairdnelson" target="_top">Laird Nelson</a>
034 *
035 * @see Events
036 */
037// Used for receiving events.
038//
039// TODO: IntEventListener, BooleanEventListener, etc.?
040//
041// <R> is almost always Void.
042// <E> is the event type.
043//
044// I know this looks like a Bean, but it is not. Specifically, the dependencies here are not thsoe required to create
045// the EventListener, but those required to receive the event.
046//
047// Given:
048//
049//   private void onEvent(@Observes /* or similar */ String e, @Complicated Frob f) {}
050//
051// * R is Void
052// * E is String
053// * dependencies() includes VariableElement-representing-f and nothing else
054// * eventDependency() includes VariableElement-representing-e and nothing else
055// * receive() calls onEvent() supplying it with event and a @Complicated Frob acquired via r
056//
057public interface EventListener<R, E> extends Aggregate, java.util.EventListener {
058
059  /**
060   * Returns a non-{@code null}, determinate, immutable {@link SequencedSet} of {@link Element}s representing
061   * dependencies this {@link EventListener} has that must be resolved before any invocation of the {@link
062   * #eventReceived(Object, ReferencesSelector2)} method may properly occur.
063   *
064   * <p>Implementations of this method must not include a result of any invocation of the {@link #eventDependency()}
065   * method as an element of the return value.</p>
066   *
067   * <p>The default implementation of this method returns an {@linkplain SequencedSet#isEmpty() empty} {@link
068   * SequencedSet}. Overrides are expected.</p>
069   *
070   * @return a non-{@code null}, determinate, immutable {@link SequencedSet} of {@link Element}s
071   *
072   * @see #eventReceived(Object, ReferencesSelector2)
073   *
074   * @see #eventDependency()
075   */
076  // Returns dependencies that are not the event dependency. These are resolved by the system. Think of an observer
077  // method with an observed parameter and other parameters. The other parameters are these dependencies.
078  @Override // Aggregate
079  public default SequencedSet<? extends Annotated<? extends Element>> dependencies() {
080    return Aggregate.super.dependencies();
081  }
082
083  /**
084   * Returns a non-{@code null}, determinate {@link Element} representing the program element for which an event is
085   * destined.
086   *
087   * <p>The result of an invocation of this method must not appear as an element of the return value of an invocation of
088   * the {@link #dependencies()} method.</p>
089   *
090   * @return a non-{@code null}, determinate {@link Element}
091   *
092   * @see #dependencies()
093   */
094  // An Element describing the event event "slot" (the observed parameter).
095  // Conceptually just another dependency (see dependencies()) but it is supplied by the user, not the system.
096  // Normally a method parameter.
097  // T's argument (the event event type) must be among its types.
098  public Annotated<? extends Element> eventDependency();
099
100  /**
101   * Receives and handles an event, normally as delivered by an invocation of the {@link Events#fire(TypeMirror, List,
102   * Object, ReferencesSelector)} method, returning the result (which is often {@link Void} ({@code null})).
103   *
104   * @param event the event; must not be {@code null}
105   *
106   * @param r a {@link ReferencesSelector} typically used to {@linkplain #assign(java.util.Function) assign} {@linkplain
107   * #dependencies() dependencies}; must not be {@code null}
108   *
109   * @return the result of reception, which may be {@code null}
110   *
111   * @exception NullPointerException if any argument is {@code null}
112   */
113  // R must be compatible with attributedType().
114  // Implementation must arrange for all dependencies() to be assigned, probably using r.
115  // Implementation must arrange for event to be assigned to eventDependency(), whatever that might mean. (Normally a method call.)
116  // Implementation must return the result of reception (usually Void, so null).
117  // When invoked by the system, r should probably also be an AutoCloseableRegistry and an AutoCloseable.
118  public R eventReceived(final E event, final ReferencesSelector r);
119  
120}
